## reference · selective attention
* Users focus on a subset of stimuli in their environment, usually those related to their goals.
* People often filter out irrelevant information to maintain focus on important tasks.
* Designers must guide users' attention and prevent overwhelm or distraction.
* **Banner Blindness**: users consciously or unconsciously ignore banner-like information on websites.
* To avoid banner blindness: don't style content like ads, and keep content and ads in separate visual sections.
* **Change Blindness**: significant interface changes can go unnoticed due to human attention limitations.
* To avoid change blindness: analyze designs for competing changes that may divert attention.

Sources: https://lawsofux.com/selective-attention/
